params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Color data from warmup. Red=40, Blue=30, Green=50, Yellow=20. Axis labeled 0–60 in 10s. Red bar highlighted with axis line at 40 glowing.

beats.text: You saw this bar graph in Warmup. Now let's learn how to read it exactly.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Color data. Red bar highlighted with axis line at 40 glowing. Axis labeled 0–60 in 10s.

params.description: Axis numbers 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60 highlight sequentially from bottom to top.

beats.text: See these numbers along the side? They show the scale. 10, 20, 30, 40. That's counting by 10s!

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Color data. Red bar highlighted. Axis numbers highlighted, showing scale of 10.

params.description: Horizontal line draws from top of Red bar to vertical axis at 40.

beats.text: Red has 40. The height of the bar ends at the 40 line.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Color data. Red bar highlighted. Guideline drawn from top of Red bar to axis at 40.

params.description: Axis labels highlight sequentially: 10, 20, 30, 40 pulse in sequence to demonstrate counting by 10s.

beats.text: I can count by 10s to check: 10, 20, 30, 40. You already know how to count by 10s!

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Same data as previous picture graph: Red=20, Blue=30, Yellow=25, Green=15. Blue bar highlighted, height at 30 axis line.

beats.text: Look at Blue. The bar's height ends at a line on the axis. What value does this show?
What value does this show?

validator.description: Student selected 30, correct bar height

beats.text: Right. 30. The bar ends at the 30 line. Same as 3 symbols on the picture graph—each symbol shows 10.

validator.description: Student selected 3, confusing the bar value with the count of intervals or symbols

params.description: Blue bar remains highlighted, axis line at 30 glows.

beats.text: Look where the bar ends. It's not at 3. Find the number on the axis where the top of the bar lines up.

validator.description: Student selected 20, reading the wrong axis value

params.description: Horizontal line draws from top of Blue bar to vertical axis at 30.

beats.text: Blue's bar goes higher than 20. Follow the top of the bar to the axis. What number is there?

validator.description: Student selected 35, the axis maximum instead of the bar height

params.description: Horizontal line draws from top of Blue bar to vertical axis at 30.

beats.text: 35 is the top of the axis, not where Blue's bar ends. Look at where the bar stops. That's the value.

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Horizontal guideline draws from top of Blue bar to axis, landing at 30. Axis label 30 pulses.

beats.text: Let me show you. The top of Blue's bar lines up with the 30 mark on the axis. That's the height of the bar. The bar shows 30.

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Four color categories. Scale of 10. Blue=40, Red=30, Green=50, Yellow=20.
Green bar highlights, height 50.

beats.text: Green shows 50. Let's count by 10s to check: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50.

params.description: Axis lines glow sequentially: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Scale of 10. Green bar highlighted at 50. Axis values visible: 0, 10, 20, 30, 40, 50.

params.description: Yellow bar highlights, height 20.

beats.text: You already know how to count by 10s. That's exactly what we do with scale of 10. The fourth bar shows Yellow. Count by 10s. What value does the bar show?
What value does the bar show?

validator.description: Student selected 20, correct

beats.text: Right. 20. Two jumps of 10: 10, 20.

validator.description: Student selected 2, counting bars instead of reading the scale

params.description: Yellow bar remains highlighted with axis gridlines emphasized.

beats.text: That's how many jumps of 10. But each jump is worth 10. Look at the axis numbers. Where does the bar end?

validator.description: Student selected 12, misreading the scale or adding incorrectly

params.description: Axis values 10, 20 highlight sequentially.

beats.text: Count by 10s from 0. First line is 10, next line is 20. Which line does Yellow's bar reach?

validator.description: Student selected 25, reading between lines when bar is at a line

params.description: Horizontal guideline draws from top of Yellow bar to axis at 20.

beats.text: The bar ends exactly at a line, not between lines. This line shows 20. Count by 10s: 10, then 20.

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Axis values 10, 20 glow in sequence, then guideline draws from Yellow bar to 20.

beats.text: Let me show you. Start at 0. Count by 10s: 10, 20. Yellow's bar ends right at the 20 line. That's the value. The answer is 20 because the bar's height matches the 20 on the axis.

params.description: Data table appears showing Favorite Recess Activities. Swings=30, Tag=50, Slides=40, Jump Rope=20.
Vertical bar graph appears in building mode. Scale of 10, axis 0–60. Ghost gridlines at 10s visible. Tag=50, Slides=40, Jump Rope=20 bars pre-filled. Swings bar empty, ready for student to set.

beats.text: Now you create a bar. Swings has 30 votes. Set the HEIGHT for Swings to show 30.
Set the HEIGHT for Swings to show 30.

validator.description: Student set Swings bar to height 30, correct

params.description: Swings bar confirms at height 30.

beats.text: Right. 10, 20, 30. The bar ends at 30 on the axis. 30 votes for Swings.

validator.description: Student set Swings bar to wrong height on first attempt

beats.text: Not quite. Find the 30 line on the axis.

validator.description: Student set Swings bar to wrong height on second attempt

params.description: Axis line at 30 highlights.

beats.text: The axis shows 10, 20, 30. Count by 10s to find 30, then set the bar there.

validator.description: Student set Swings bar to wrong height three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System sets Swings bar to height 30.

beats.text: Let me show you. I count by 10s on the axis: 10, 20, 30. The Swings bar ends at 30, right at that line. That's how we show 30 votes.

params.description: Data table appears. Books Read This Month. Fiction=40, Nonfiction=60, Comics=20, Poetry=30. Horizontal layout, numeric display.
Vertical bar graph appears. Books Read This Month. Scale of 10, axis 0–70. Fiction bar at 40, Comics at 20, Poetry at 30. Nonfiction bar empty. Ghost gridlines at 10s visible. Snap-to-10 enabled.

beats.text: Nonfiction has 60 books. That's the biggest number. Set the bar for Nonfiction.
Set the bar for Nonfiction to show 60.

validator.description: Student set Nonfiction bar to 60, correct

params.description: Nonfiction bar confirms at 60.

beats.text: Right. Nonfiction at 60. You found the 60 line and set the bar there.

validator.description: Student set Nonfiction bar to wrong height on first attempt

beats.text: Not quite. Look at the data table. What number does Nonfiction show?

validator.description: Student set Nonfiction bar to wrong height on second attempt

params.description: Nonfiction bar area highlights to guide placement.

beats.text: Nonfiction has 60 books. Count by 10s up the axis to find where 60 is. Set the bar at that line.

validator.description: Student set Nonfiction bar to wrong height three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System sets Nonfiction bar to 60.

beats.text: Let me show you. I count by 10s: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60. The bar ends at the 60 line on the axis. Nonfiction has 60 books, so the bar goes to 60.

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Books Read This Month data. Fiction=40, Nonfiction=60, Comics=20, Poetry=30. All 4 bars visible.

beats.text: Here's the completed graph. Use it to answer a question. How many MORE books were Nonfiction than Comics?
How many MORE books were Nonfiction than Comics?

validator.description: Student selected 40, correct difference between Nonfiction (60) and Comics (20)

beats.text: Right. Nonfiction is 60, Comics is 20. The difference is 40.

validator.description: Student selected 20, the value of Comics instead of the difference

params.description: Nonfiction and Comics bars highlight.

beats.text: The question asks how many MORE. Find the difference between Nonfiction and Comics. What are the two values?

validator.description: Student selected 60, the value of Nonfiction instead of the difference

params.description: Nonfiction and Comics bars highlight.

beats.text: You found Nonfiction. But the question asks how many MORE than Comics. Subtract Comics from Nonfiction to find the difference.

validator.description: Student selected 80, the sum instead of the difference

params.description: Nonfiction and Comics bars highlight.

beats.text: You added Nonfiction and Comics. The question asks how many MORE, not the total. Find the difference by subtracting.

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Visual shows Nonfiction at 60, Comics at 20, with the gap between them highlighted to show difference of 40.

beats.text: Let me show you. Nonfiction is at 60. Comics is at 20. To find how many MORE, I subtract: 60 minus 20 equals 40. The difference between the two bars is 40 books.

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Favorite Colors data. Blue bar ends between grid lines.

beats.text: You can read bars that end right at the lines. But what if a bar ends between the lines?

params.title: Stickers Collected
params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Stickers Collected. Emma=30 (at 30 line), Jake=25 (between 20-30 lines), Mia=40 (at 40 line), Leo=35 (between 30-40 lines). Scale of 10, axis 0-50. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible (lighter than 10s lines). Jake's bar highlighted.

beats.text: Look at Jake's bar. It doesn't end at a line. It ends between the lines.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph in reading mode. Stickers Collected. Jake's bar highlighted, ending between 20 and 30 lines. Ghost gridlines visible at 5s.
elements.title: Stickers Collected

beats.text: Remember half-symbols from scale of 2? Half of 2 was 1. Same idea here. Half of 10 is 5.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph in reading mode. Stickers Collected. Jake's bar highlighted, ending between 20 and 30 lines. Ghost gridlines visible at 5s.
elements.title: Stickers Collected

beats.text: Half of 10 is 5. So between 20 and 30, that's 20 + 5 = 25. Jake has 25 stickers.

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Stickers Collected data. Leo's bar height = 35 (between 30 and 40 lines). Ghost gridlines at 5s visible. Leo's bar highlighted.

beats.text: Now look at Leo's bar. It's also between the lines. Leo's bar is between 30 and 40. What value does this show?
What value does Leo's bar show?

validator.description: Student selected 35, correct, halfway between 30 and 40

beats.text: Right. 35. It's halfway between 30 and 40. Half of 10 is 5, so 30 plus 5 is 35.

validator.description: Student selected 30, the lower bound instead of the halfway point

params.description: Leo's bar highlighted with ghost gridline at 35 pulsing.

beats.text: Leo's bar doesn't stop at 30. It goes past that line. Look where the bar ends.

validator.description: Student selected 34, close but not aligned with scale of 5

params.description: Leo's bar highlighted. Ghost gridlines at 30 and 40 pulse, then 35 gridline pulses.

beats.text: This graph counts by 5s. Between 30 and 40, halfway is 35, not 34.

validator.description: Student selected 40, the upper bound instead of the halfway point

params.description: Leo's bar highlighted. Gridline at 40 highlighted separately to show gap.

beats.text: Leo's bar doesn't reach 40. It stops between 30 and 40. Where is halfway?

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Axis lines glow sequentially: 30 pulses, then 35 pulses at the top of Leo's bar, showing it ends halfway to 40.

beats.text: Let me show you. Leo's bar is between 30 and 40. Halfway between those two lines is 35. Half of 10 is 5, so 30 plus 5 equals 35. That's where Leo's bar ends.

params.description: Data table appears. Points Scored data: Team A=45, Team B=30, Team C=25, Team D=50, Team E=15.

beats.text: 45 is halfway between 40 and 50. Find the middle point between those two lines and place the bar there.

validator.description: Student set Team A bar to wrong height three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System sets Team A bar to height 45, demonstrating correct placement halfway between 40 and 50.

beats.text: Let me show you. I look at the axis: 40 is one line, 50 is the next line. Halfway between them is 45. I place the bar so it ends exactly there, in the middle. That's how we show 45 on a scale of 10.

params.description: Data table appears. Laps Completed data: Runner A=20, Runner B=50, Runner C=35, Runner D=45. Horizontal layout.

validator.description: Student set Runner C bar to wrong height three or more times. System models the answer.

Half of 10 is 5, so 30 plus 5 is 35. The bar ends at 35. That's how you match the bar height to the data when the value is between the lines.

params.description: Data table appears showing Minutes of Reading values: Monday=40, Tuesday=35, Wednesday=50, Thursday=25.
Vertical bar graph appears with scale of 10, axis 0 to 60. No bars pre-filled. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible. Snap-to-5 enabled.

Complete the graph.

validator.description: Student set all four bars correctly: Monday=40, Tuesday=35, Wednesday=50, Thursday=25

beats.text: Right. Monday and Wednesday are at the axis lines. Tuesday and Thursday are between the lines. You figured out which is which.

validator.description: Student did not set all bars correctly on first attempt

validator.description: Student did not set all bars correctly on second attempt

params.description: Monday and Wednesday bars highlight.

validator.description: Student did not set all bars correctly. System models the answer.

params.description: System sets all four bars in sequence: Monday at 40, Tuesday at 35, Wednesday at 50, Thursday at 25.

beats.text: Let me show you. Monday is 40, right at the 40 line. Tuesday is 35, halfway between 30 and 40. Wednesday is 50, at the 50 line. Thursday is 25, halfway between 20 and 30. Values ending in 0 land on the lines. Values ending in 5 land between the lines.

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Minutes of Reading data. Scale of 10. Monday=40 (at line), Tuesday=35 (between lines), Wednesday=50 (at line), Thursday=25 (between lines).

How many TOTAL minutes of reading for Monday and Tuesday?

validator.description: Student selected 75, correct total

validator.description: Student selected 35, only Tuesday's value

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Monday bar highlights showing 40, then Tuesday bar highlights showing 35.

beats.text: Let me show you how to find the total. Monday's bar ends at 40. Tuesday's bar ends halfway between 30 and 40, so that's 35. When we add them together, 40 plus 35 equals 75. The answer is 75 minutes total.

Each bar ends at its value, whether at a line or between. The graph shows all four: Kai=35, Ava=50, Noah=25, Zoe=45.
